title: Stock Span Problem
description: Financial trading systems compute the 'span' of a stock — the number of consecutive preceding days where the price was less than or equal to today's price. A monotonic decreasing stack tracks indices of dominant prices, enabling O(1) amortized span calculation as each price is pushed and weaker prices are popped.

title: Histogram Largest Rectangle
description: Graphics and layout engines compute the largest rectangle that fits within a histogram of bar heights. A monotonic increasing stack maintains bars in ascending order; when a shorter bar is encountered, the stack unwinds to calculate the maximum area achievable with each popped bar as the shortest in its span.
